zh.lifeSummary: 當Roger I De Berkeley Lord of Gloucestershire於1040年4月1日出生在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾度斯利時，他的父親William de Berkeley I29歲，母親Elizabeth de Betteshorne21歲。他於大約1064年與Rissa Montgomery在英國格盧斯特斯爾結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1093年9月3日在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾貝克萊享年53歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾格盧斯特 凱德拉爾。

nameMeanings.name: Berkeley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from one or more of the numerous places so named, such as Berkeley in Gloucestershire, named in Old English with be(o)rc ‘birch’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’. Compare Scottish Barclay .
Jewish (American): adoption of the English surname (see 1 above) in place of   Berkowitz  .

variants.name: Barclay
meanings: Scottish: habitational name of English origin, from any of several places so called, such as Berkley in Somerset or Berkeley in Gloucestershire, both named in Old English with be(o)rc ‘birch’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’.

History: The surname was brought to Scotland from southwestern England in the 12th century; Walter de Berchelai or Berkelai was Chamberlain of Scotland in 1165. It was long assumed that they were from the family that held Berkeley Castle in Gloucestershire, but the historian G. W. S. Barrow (1973, The Kingdom of the Scots) adduced evidence that they are more probably from Berkley in Somerset.

variants.name: Berkley
meanings: English: variant of   Berkeley  . Compare Scottish   Barclay  .

nameMeanings.name: Roger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name, Rog(i)er, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hrōd ‘fame’ + gār, gēr ‘spear’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, replacing the native Old English form Hrōðgār. Roger was one of the most popular boys' names throughout the medieval period, but less so after the Reformation, though it has continued in regular use to the present day. Roger, Count of Sicily ( c.1031–1101 ), son of Tancred, recovered Sicily from the Arabs. His son, also called Roger, ruled Sicily as king, presiding over a court noted for its splendour and patronage of the arts.

variants.name: Rodger
meanings: Variant spelling of   Roger  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

en.lifeSummary: When Roger I De Berkeley Lord of Gloucestershire was born on 1 April 1040, in Dursley, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom, his father, William de Berkeley I, was 29 and his mother, Elizabeth de Betteshorne, was 21. He married Rissa Montgomery about 1064, in Gloucestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 3 September 1093, in Berkeley, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 53, and was buried in Gloucester Cathedral, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Roger I De Berkeley Lord of Gloucestershire est né le 1 avril 1040, à Dursley, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William de Berkeley I, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth de Betteshorne, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Rissa Montgomery environ 1064, à Gloucestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 3 septembre 1093, à Berkeley, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 53 ans, et a été enterré à Gloucester Cathedral, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.Gloucester Cathedral, Gloucester, Glouce: Gloucester Cathedral, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als Roger I De Berkeley Lord of Gloucestershire am 1. April 1040 in Dursley, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater William de Berkeley I 29 und seine Mutter Elizabeth de Betteshorne 21. Er hat um 1064 in Gloucestershire, England Rissa Montgomery geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 3. September 1093 im Alter von 53 Jahren in Berkeley, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Gloucester Cathedral, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

Lui sposato Rissa Montgomery circa 1064, nel Gloucestershire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 3 settembre 1093, nel Berkeley, Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 53 anni, e fu sepolto a Gloucester Cathedral, Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Roger I De Berkeley Lord of Gloucestershireが1040年4月1日にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアダルスリーで生まれた時，父William de Berkeley Iは29歳，母Elizabeth de Betteshorneは21歳であった。約1064年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアでRissa Montgomeryと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1093年9月3日にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアバーケリーで53歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアグロウセスター カスエドラルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Roger I De Berkeley Lord of Gloucestershire(이)가 1040년 4월 1일 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 뒤르슬리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William de Berkeley I(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth de Betteshorne(은)는 21세였다. 그는 대략 1064년 영국 그루스스터실르에서 Rissa Montgomery(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1093년 9월 3일 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 버켈리에서 53세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 그루스스터 캐테드럴에 묻혔다.

pt.Gloucester Cathedral, Gloucester, Glouce: Gloucester Cathedral, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Roger I De Berkeley Lord of Gloucestershire nasceu em 1 de abril de 1040, em Dursley, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, William de Berkeley I, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth de Betteshorne, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Rissa Montgomery aproximadamente 1064, em Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 3 de setembro de 1093, em Berkeley, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 53 anos, e foi sepultado em Gloucester Cathedral, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Roger I De Berkeley Lord of Gloucestershire родился 1 апреля 1040 г. в местности дурсли, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени William de Berkeley I было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth de Betteshorne, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Rissa Montgomery около 1064 г. в местности глоусестиршир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 2 дочери. Он умер 3 сентября 1093 г. в местности биркели, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 53 лет и был похоронен в местности глоусестир кэтедраль, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания.
